Kikuyu: “Ũrĩa wothe ũkaanyumbũra mbere ya andũ, o na niĩ nĩngamumbũra mbere ya Baba ũrĩa ũrĩ kũu igũrũ.
English: Everyone therefore who confesses me before men, I will also confess him before my Father who is in heaven.

Kikuyu: No ũrĩa wothe ũkangaana mbere ya andũ, na niĩ nĩngamũkaana mbere ya Baba ũrĩa ũrĩ igũrũ.
English: But whoever denies me before men, I will also deny him before my Father who is in heaven.

Kikuyu: “Mũtigeciirie atĩ njũkĩte kũrehe thayũ gũkũ thĩ. Ndiokire kũrehe thayũ, no ndookire kũrehe rũhiũ rwa njora.
English: “Don’t think that I came to send peace on the earth. I didn’t come to send peace, but a sword.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo njũkĩte gũtũma “ ‘mũndũ okanĩrĩre na ithe, na mũirĩtu okanĩrĩre na nyina, * 10:14 Kũribariba rũkũngũ magũrũ nĩ ũndũ wekagwo nĩ Afarisai moima ciikaro cia Ndũrĩrĩ metherie tondũ metĩkĩtie nĩmakuuĩte thaahu. Ũndũ ũyũ warĩ wa kuonia andũ ũrĩa mageekwo marega ndũmĩrĩri ya Ngai. † 10:25 Rĩĩtwa Beelizebuli rĩgĩtaũrwo nĩ kuuga “Mũnene wa ndaimono,” no Kĩhibirania Beelizebubu rĩataũragwo “Mũnene wa ngi.”
English: For I came to set a man at odds against his father, and a daughter against her mother, and a daughter-in-law against her mother-in-law.

Kikuyu: thũ cia mũndũ igaakorwo irĩ andũ a nyũmba yake kĩũmbe.’
English: A man’s foes will be those of his own household.*

Kikuyu: “Ũrĩa wothe wendete ithe kana nyina kũngĩra ndagĩrĩire gũtuĩka wakwa; na ũrĩa wothe wendete mũriũ kana mwarĩ kũngĩra ndagĩrĩire gũtuĩka wakwa;
English: He who loves father or mother more than me is not worthy of me; and he who loves son or daughter more than me isn’t worthy of me.

Kikuyu: nake ũrĩa wothe ũtagakuua mũtharaba wake wa kwambĩrwo aanũmĩrĩre ndagĩrĩire gũtuĩka wakwa.
English: He who doesn’t take his cross and follow after me isn’t worthy of me.

Kikuyu: Ũrĩa wothe ũgeragia gũtũũria muoyo wake nĩakoorwo nĩguo; no ũrĩa wothe ũkoorwo nĩ muoyo wake nĩ ũndũ wakwa nĩakaũtũũria.
English: He who seeks his life will lose it; and he who loses his life for my sake will find it.

Kikuyu: “Mũndũ ũrĩa ũmwamũkagĩra, nĩ niĩ amũkagĩra. Nake ũrĩa ũnyamũkagĩra, amũkagĩra ũrĩa wandũmire.
English: “He who receives you receives me, and he who receives me receives him who sent me.

Kikuyu: Ũrĩa wothe ũkaamũkĩra mũnabii tondũ nĩ mũnabii, nĩakamũkĩra kĩheo kĩrĩa kĩheagwo mũnabii, nake ũrĩa wothe ũkaamũkĩra mũndũ mũthingu tondũ nĩ mũthingu-rĩ, nĩakamũkĩra kĩheo kĩrĩa kĩheagwo mũndũ mũthingu.
English: He who receives a prophet in the name of a prophet will receive a prophet’s reward. He who receives a righteous man in the name of a righteous man will receive a righteous man’s reward.

Kikuyu: Nake ũrĩa wothe ũheaga ũmwe wa aya anini o na gĩkombe kĩa maaĩ mahehu tondũ nĩ mũrutwo wakwa-rĩ, ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, ndakaaga kũheo kĩheo gĩake.”
English: Whoever gives one of these little ones just a cup of cold water to drink in the name of a disciple, most certainly I tell you, he will in no way lose his reward.”

Kikuyu: a rĩrĩ, Jesũ aarĩkia gwatha arutwo acio ake ikũmi na eerĩ-rĩ, akiuma kũu agĩthiĩ kũrutana na kũhunjia matũũra-inĩ ma Galili.
English: hen Jesus had finished directing his twelve disciples, he departed from there to teach and preach in their cities.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa Johana aiguĩre arĩ njeera maũndũ marĩa Kristũ eekaga-rĩ, agĩtũma arutwo ake,
English: Now when John heard in the prison the works of Christ, he sent two of his disciples

Kikuyu: makamũũrie atĩrĩ, “Wee nĩwe warĩ ũũke, kana tweterere mũndũ ũngĩ?”
English: and said to him, “Are you he who comes, or should we look for another?”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Cookai mũkeere Johana maũndũ marĩa mwaigua na marĩa muona:
English: Jesus answered them, “Go and tell John the things which you hear and see:

Kikuyu: Atumumu nĩmaroona, na arĩa mathuaga nĩmaretwara, na arĩa marĩ na mangũ nĩmarahonio magathera, na arĩa mataiguaga nĩ maraigua, na arĩa akuũ nĩmarariũkio, na Ũhoro-ũrĩa-Mwega nĩũrahunjĩrio arĩa athĩĩni.
English: the blind receive their sight, the lame walk, the lepers are cleansed, the deaf hear,* the dead are raised up, and the poor have good news preached to them.*

Kikuyu: Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa ũtekũhĩngwo arakare nĩ ũndũ wakwa.”
English: Blessed is he who finds no occasion for stumbling in me.”

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa arutwo a Johana moimaga hau, Jesũ akĩambĩrĩria kwarĩria kĩrĩndĩ ũhoro wa Johana, akĩmooria atĩrĩ: “Mwathiĩte werũ-inĩ kuona kĩ? Mwathiĩte kuona kamũrangi kainainagio nĩ rũhuho?
English: As these went their way, Jesus began to say to the multitudes concerning John, “What did you go out into the wilderness to see? A reed shaken by the wind?

Kikuyu: Akorwo ti ũguo-rĩ, mwathiĩte kuona kĩ? Hihi nĩ mũndũ wehumbĩte nguo njega? Aca, arĩa mehumbaga nguo njega makoragwo thĩinĩ wa nyũmba cia ũthamaki.
English: But what did you go out to see? A man in soft clothing? Behold, those who wear soft clothing are in kings’ houses.

Kikuyu: Mwagĩthiĩte kuona kĩ? Hihi mwathiĩte kuona mũnabii? Ĩĩ, ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, muonire mũndũ ũkĩrĩte mũnabii.
English: But why did you go out? To see a prophet? Yes, I tell you, and much more than a prophet.

Kikuyu: Ũyũ nĩwe ũhoro wake waandĩkĩtwo atĩrĩ: “ ‘Nĩngũtũma mũtũmwo wakwa athiĩ mbere yaku, agagũthondekere njĩra yaku.’
English: For this is he, of whom it is written, ‘Behold, I send my messenger before your face, who will prepare your way before you.’*

Kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma: Harĩ andũ arĩa manaciarwo nĩ andũ-a-nja, gũtirĩ kuoneka mũndũ mũnene gũkĩra Johana Mũbatithania; no rĩrĩ mũndũ ũrĩa mũnini mũno ũthamaki-inĩ wa igũrũ-rĩ, nĩ mũnene kũmũkĩra.
English: Most certainly I tell you, among those who are born of women there has not arisen anyone greater than John the Baptizer; yet he who is least in the Kingdom of Heaven is greater than he.

Kikuyu: Kuuma matukũ ma Johana Mũbatithania gwata rĩu, ũthamaki wa igũrũ nĩũkoretwo ũkĩĩhatĩrĩria gũtheerema, nao andũ arĩa mehatagĩrĩria nĩo mawĩgwatagĩra na hinya.
English: From the days of John the Baptizer until now, the Kingdom of Heaven suffers violence, and the violent take it by force.†

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Anabii othe, hamwe na Watho wa Musa, nĩmatũire marathaga ũhoro nginya hĩndĩ ya Johana.
English: For all the prophets and the law prophesied until John.

Kikuyu: Na angĩkorwo no mwende kwamũkĩra ũhoro ũcio-rĩ, ũcio nĩwe Elija ũrĩa werĩtwo nĩagooka.
English: If you are willing to receive it, this is Elijah, who is to come.

Kikuyu: Ũrĩa ũrĩ na matũ ma kũigua nĩaigue.
English: He who has ears to hear, let him hear.

Kikuyu: “Rĩu-rĩ, ingĩgerekania rũciaro rũrũ na kĩ? Rũhaana ta ciana ciikarĩte ndũnyũ igĩĩtaga iria ingĩ, igaciĩra atĩrĩ:
English: “But to what shall I compare this generation? It is like children sitting in the marketplaces, who call to their companions

Kikuyu: “ ‘Twamũhuhĩire mũtũrirũ, mũkĩaga kũina; o na twamũinĩire nyĩmbo cia macakaya, no mũtiacakaire.’
English: and say, ‘We played the flute for you, and you didn’t dance. We mourned for you, and you didn’t lament.’  An assarion is a small coin worth one tenth of a drachma or a sixteenth of a denarius. An assarion is approximately the wages of one half hour of agricultural labor.  Micah 7:6  Isaiah 35:5

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Johana ookire atekũrĩa na atekũnyua, nao andũ makiuga atĩrĩ, ‘Arĩ na ndaimono.’
English: For John came neither eating nor drinking, and they say, ‘He has a demon.’

Kikuyu: Mũrũ wa Mũndũ ookire akĩrĩĩaga na akĩnyuuaga, nao makiuga atĩrĩ, ‘Onei mũndũ mũkoroku na mũrĩĩu, nĩ mũrata wa etia mbeeca cia igooti na “andũ ehia”.’ No ũũgĩ wonekaga nĩ wa ma nĩ ũndũ wa ciĩko ciaguo.” Haaro kũrĩ Matũũra Manene marĩa Materirĩte
English: The Son of Man came eating and drinking, and they say, ‘Behold, a gluttonous man and a drunkard, a friend of tax collectors and sinners!’ But wisdom is justified by her children.”‡

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Jesũ akĩambĩrĩria gũtetia matũũra manene marĩa maaringĩirwo ciama ciake iria nyingĩ, tondũ andũ akuo matiigana kwĩrira, akiuga atĩrĩ,
English: Then he began to denounce the cities in which most of his mighty works had been done, because they didn’t repent.

Kikuyu: “Inyuĩ andũ a Korazini, kaĩ mũrĩ na haaro-ĩ!
English: “Woe to you, Chorazin! Woe to you, Bethsaida! For if the mighty works had been done in Tyre and Sidon which were done in you, they would have repented long ago in sackcloth and ashes.

Kikuyu: No ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, andũ a Turo na Sidoni nĩmagakorwo marĩ murũ mũthenya ũcio wa ciira gũkĩra inyuĩ.
English: But I tell you, it will be more tolerable for Tyre and Sidon on the day of judgment than for you.

Kikuyu: Na inyuĩ, andũ a Kaperinaumu, nĩmũkambarario nginya matu-inĩ? Aca, mũkaaharũrũkio mũkinye kũrĩa kũriku mũno. Ciama iria mwaringĩirwo ingĩaringĩirwo Sodomu, nĩkũngĩtũũraga nginya ũmũthĩ.
English: You, Capernaum, who are exalted to heaven, you will go down to Hades. § For if the mighty works had been done in Sodom which were done in you, it would have remained until today.

Kikuyu: No ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, Sodomu nĩgũgakorwo murũ mũthenya ũcio wa ciira kũmũkĩra.” Kũhurũka kwa arĩa anogu
English: But I tell you that it will be more tolerable for the land of Sodom on the day of judgment, than for you.”

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Jesũ akiuga atĩrĩ, “Baba, wee Mwathani wa igũrũ na thĩ, nĩngũkũgaatha tondũ nĩũhithĩte andũ arĩa oogĩ na marĩ na ũmenyo maũndũ maya, na ũkamaguũrĩria tũkenge.
English: At that time, Jesus answered, “I thank you, Father, Lord of heaven and earth, that you hid these things from the wise and understanding, and revealed them to infants.

Kikuyu: Ĩĩ, Baba, nĩgũkorwo ũyũ nĩ wendi waku.
English: Yes, Father, for so it was well-pleasing in your sight.

Kikuyu: “Nĩnengeretwo maũndũ mothe nĩ Baba. Gũtirĩ mũndũ ũũĩ Mũriũ tiga Ithe, na gũtirĩ mũndũ ũũĩ Ithe tiga Mũriũ, na arĩa Mũriũ, endaga kũguũrĩria.
English: All things have been delivered to me by my Father. No one knows the Son, except the Father; neither does anyone know the Father, except the Son and he to whom the Son desires to reveal him.

Kikuyu: “Ũkai kũrĩ niĩ, inyuothe anogu na mũkuuĩte mĩrigo mĩritũ, na nĩngũmũhurũkia.
English: “Come to me, all you who labor and are heavily burdened, and I will give you rest.

Kikuyu: Oyai icooki rĩakwa mwĩigĩrĩre na mwĩrute na niĩ, nĩgũkorwo ndĩ mũhooreri na mwĩnyiihia ngoro-inĩ, nacio ngoro cianyu nĩikũhurũkio.
English: Take my yoke upon you and learn from me, for I am gentle and humble in heart; and you will find rest for your souls.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo icooki rĩakwa nĩ ihũthũ, na mũrigo wakwa nĩ mũhũthũ.”
English: For my yoke is easy, and my burden is light.”

Kikuyu: hinda rĩu Jesũ nĩatuĩkanĩirie mĩgũnda-inĩ ya ngano mũthenya wa Thabatũ, na arutwo ake maarĩ ahũtu, na makĩambĩrĩria gũtua ngiracia ngano, magĩcirigitha, makĩrĩa ngano.
English: t that time, Jesus went on the Sabbath day through the grain fields. His disciples were hungry and began to pluck heads of grain and to eat.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Afarisai moonire ũguo, makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ta rora! Arutwo aku nĩmareeka ũrĩa watho ũtetĩkĩrĩtie gwĩkwo mũthenya wa Thabatũ.”
English: But the Pharisees, when they saw it, said to him, “Behold, your disciples do what is not lawful to do on the Sabbath.”

Kikuyu: Nake akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Kaĩ mũtathomete ũrĩa Daudi eekire rĩrĩa we na arĩa maarĩ nake maarĩ ahũtu?
English: But he said to them, “Haven’t you read what David did when he was hungry, and those who were with him:

Kikuyu: Aatoonyire nyũmba ya Ngai, na arĩ hamwe na arĩa maarĩ nake, makĩrĩa mĩgate ĩrĩa yamũrĩirwo Ngai ĩrĩa mateetĩkĩrĩtio kũrĩa nĩ watho, tiga no athĩnjĩri-Ngai meetĩkĩrĩtio kũmĩrĩa.
English: how he entered into God’s house and ate the show bread, which was not lawful for him to eat, nor for those who were with him, but only for the priests?*

Kikuyu: O na ningĩ kaĩ mũthomete Watho-inĩ atĩ mũthenya wa Thabatũ, athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa marĩ thĩinĩ wa hekarũ nĩmathaahagia mũthenya ũcio na matituagwo ahĩtia?
English: Or have you not read in the law that on the Sabbath day the priests in the temple profane the Sabbath and are guiltless?

Kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, ũmwe mũnene gũkĩra hekarũ arĩ haha.
English: But I tell you that one greater than the temple is here.

Kikuyu: Korwo nĩmwamenyete gĩtũmi gĩa ciugo ici, atĩrĩ, ‘Kũiguanĩra tha nĩkuo nyendaga, no ti magongona,’ mũtingĩatuĩrĩire andũ mateehĩtie mahĩtia.
English: But if you had known what this means, ‘I desire mercy, and not sacrifice,’* you wouldn’t have condemned the guiltless.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Mũrũ wa Mũndũ nĩwe Mwathani wa mũthenya wa Thabatũ.”
English: For the Son of Man is Lord of the Sabbath.”

Kikuyu: Oima kũu-rĩ, agĩthiĩ agĩtoonya thunagogi yao,
English: He departed from there and went into their synagogue.

Kikuyu: na nĩ haarĩ mũndũ wonjete guoko. Na tondũ nĩmacaragia gĩtũmi gĩa gũthitangĩra Jesũ-rĩ, makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Watho nĩwĩtĩkĩrĩtie kũhonania mũthenya wa Thabatũ?”
English: And behold, there was a man with a withered hand. They asked him, “Is it lawful to heal on the Sabbath day?” so that they might accuse him.

Kikuyu: Nake akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Nũũ wanyu ũngĩkorwo na ngʼondu, nayo ĩgwe irima mũthenya wa Thabatũ-rĩ, ũtangĩmĩruta irima?
English: He said to them, “What man is there among you who has one sheep, and if this one falls into a pit on the Sabbath day, won’t he grab on to it and lift it out?

Kikuyu: Kaĩ mũndũ atakĩrĩ wa bata mũno gũkĩra ngʼondu! Nĩ ũndũ ũcio watho nĩwĩtĩkĩrĩtie gwĩka wega mũthenya wa Thabatũ.”
English: Of how much more value then is a man than a sheep! Therefore it is lawful to do good on the Sabbath day.”

Kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩĩra mũndũ ũcio atĩrĩ, “Tambũrũkia guoko gwaku.” Nake agĩtambũrũkia guoko, nakuo gũgĩtuĩka kũgima, o ta kũu kũngĩ.
English: Then he told the man, “Stretch out your hand.” He stretched it out; and it was restored whole, just like the other.

Kikuyu: No Afarisai makiuma nja, magĩcookania ndundu ya ũrĩa mangĩũragithia Jesũ. Ndungata ya Ngai ĩrĩa Thuure
English: But the Pharisees went out and conspired against him, how they might destroy him.

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ aamenya ũhoro ũcio, akĩehera kũu. Andũ aingĩ makĩmũrũmĩrĩra, nake akĩhonia arũaru ao othe,
English: Jesus, perceiving that, withdrew from there. Great multitudes followed him; and he healed them all,

Kikuyu: agĩcooka akĩmakaania atĩ matikoige we nĩwe ũ.
English: and commanded them that they should not make him known,

Kikuyu: Ũndũ ũyũ nĩguo wahingirie ũhoro ũrĩa waarĩtio na kanua ka mũnabii Isaia, rĩrĩa oigire atĩrĩ:
English: that it might be fulfilled which was spoken through Isaiah the prophet, saying,

Kikuyu: “Ĩno nĩyo ndungata yakwa ĩrĩa ndĩĩthuurĩire, ĩrĩa nyendete, na nĩyo ĩkenagia ngoro yakwa; nĩngamĩitĩrĩria Roho wakwa, nayo nĩĩkamenyithia ndũrĩrĩ ciira wa kĩhooto.
English: “Behold, my servant whom I have chosen, my beloved in whom my soul is well pleased. I will put my Spirit on him. He will proclaim justice to the nations.

Kikuyu: Ndĩgaatetania kana ĩgũũthũke;
English: He will not strive, nor shout, neither will anyone hear his voice in the streets.

Kikuyu: ndĩkoina kamũrangi kagondoku, kana ĩhorie rũtambĩ rũrĩa rũgũtooga, nginya rĩrĩa ĩgaatũma itua rĩakwa rĩhootane.
English: He won’t break a bruised reed. He won’t quench a smoking flax, until he leads justice to victory.

Kikuyu: Thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩayo ndũrĩrĩ nĩikaagĩa na kĩĩrĩgĩrĩro.” Jesũ na Beelizebuli
English: In his name, the nations will hope.”*

Kikuyu: Magĩcooka makĩmũrehera mũndũ warĩ na ndaimono, na aarĩ mũtumumu, na ndaaragia, nake Jesũ akĩmũhonia, akĩhota kwaria na kuona.
English: Then one possessed by a demon, blind and mute, was brought to him; and he healed him, so that the blind and mute man both spoke and saw.

Kikuyu: Andũ othe makĩgega, makĩũrania atĩrĩ, “Ũyũ no akorwo arĩ we Mũrũ wa Daudi?”
English: All the multitudes were amazed, and said, “Can this be the son of David?”

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩa Afarisai maaiguire ũguo, makiuga atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũyũ aingataga ndaimono na hinya wa Beelizebuli ũrĩa mũnene wa ndaimono.”
English: But when the Pharisees heard it, they said, “This man does not cast out demons except by Beelzebul, the prince of the demons.”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ aamenya ũrĩa meciiragia, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ũthamaki o wothe ũngĩgayũkana ũũkanĩrĩre guo mwene no ũharagane, narĩo itũũra o rĩothe kana nyũmba ĩngĩgayũkana ndĩngĩĩhaanda.
English: Knowing their thoughts, Jesus said to them, “Every kingdom divided against itself is brought to desolation, and every city or house divided against itself will not stand.

Kikuyu: Angĩkorwo Shaitani nĩwe ũingataga Shaitani-rĩ, nĩagayũkanĩte we mwene. Ũthamaki wake ũngĩkĩĩhaanda atĩa?
English: If Satan casts out Satan, he is divided against himself. How then will his kingdom stand?

Kikuyu: Na angĩkorwo niĩ nyiingataga ndaimono na hinya wa Beelizebuli-rĩ, nao andũ anyu maingataga ndaimono na hinya wa ũ? Nĩ ũndũ wa ũguo-rĩ, acio nĩo makaamũtuĩra ciira.
English: If I by Beelzebul cast out demons, by whom do your children cast them out? Therefore they will be your judges.

Kikuyu: No niĩ ingĩkorwo nyiingataga ndaimono na Roho wa Ngai-rĩ, ũthamaki wa Ngai nĩũkinyĩte kũrĩ inyuĩ.
English: But if I by the Spirit of God cast out demons, then God’s Kingdom has come upon you.

Kikuyu: “Ningĩ-rĩ, mũndũ aahota atĩa gũtoonya nyũmba ya mũndũ njamba na amũtunye indo ciake ataambĩte kuoha mũndũ ũcio njamba? Hĩndĩ ĩyo no rĩo angĩhota kũmũtunya indo cia nyũmba yake.
English: Or how can one enter into the house of the strong man and plunder his goods, unless he first bind the strong man? Then he will plunder his house.

Kikuyu: “Mũndũ ũrĩa ũtarĩ hamwe na niĩ, ũcio nĩ thũ yakwa, nake ũrĩa ũtacookanagĩrĩria hamwe na niĩ, ũcio nĩkũhurunja ahurunjaga.
English: “He who is not with me is against me, and he who doesn’t gather with me, scatters.

Kikuyu: Ningĩ ngũmwĩra atĩ, rĩĩhia o rĩothe o na kũruma Ngai, andũ no marekerwo, no mũndũ ũrĩa ũkaaruma Roho, ũcio ndakarekerwo.
English: Therefore I tell you, every sin and blasphemy will be forgiven men, but the blasphemy against the Spirit will not be forgiven men.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ o wothe ũrĩa ũgacaambia Mũrũ wa Mũndũ nĩakarekerwo, no ũrĩa wothe ũgaacambia Roho Mũtheru, ũcio ndakaarekerwo, ihinda rĩrĩ tũrĩ kana ihinda rĩrĩa rĩgooka.
English: Whoever speaks a word against the Son of Man, it will be forgiven him; but whoever speaks against the Holy Spirit, it will not be forgiven him, either in this age, or in that which is to come.

Kikuyu: “Agĩriai mũtĩ na maciaro maguo nĩmekwagĩra, kana mũthũkie mũtĩ na mũthũkie maciaro maguo mooru, nĩgũkorwo mũtĩ ũmenyekaga na maciaro maguo.
English: “Either make the tree good and its fruit good, or make the tree corrupt and its fruit corrupt; for the tree is known by its fruit.

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ rũciaro rũrũ rwa nduĩra, mwahota atĩa kwaria maũndũ mega, na mũrĩ ooru? Nĩgũkorwo kanua kaaragia maũndũ marĩa maiyũrĩte ngoro- inĩ.
English: You offspring of vipers, how can you, being evil, speak good things? For out of the abundance of the heart, the mouth speaks.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ mwega aaragia maũndũ mega moimĩte mũthiithũ-inĩ wake mwega, nake mũndũ mũũru aaragia maũndũ mooru moimĩte mũthiithũ-inĩ wake mũũru.
English: The good man out of his good treasure† brings out good things, and the evil man out of his evil treasure brings out evil things.

Kikuyu: No ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, mũthenya wa itua rĩa ciira, andũ nĩmakaheana ũhoro wa ciugo ciothe cia tũhũ iria manaaria.
English: I tell you that every idle word that men speak, they will give account of it in the day of judgment.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo ciugo ciaku nĩcio igaatũma ũtuuo ndũrĩ na mahĩtia, na ciugo ciaku no cio igaatũma ũtuuo mũhĩtia.” Kĩama kĩa Jona
English: For by your words you will be justified, and by your words you will be condemned.”

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Afarisai amwe na arutani a watho makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Mũrutani, tũkwenda ũtũringĩre kĩama.”
English: Then certain of the scribes and Pharisees answered, “Teacher, we want to see a sign from you.”

Kikuyu: Nake akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Rũciaro rwaganu na rwa ũtharia rũrenda rũringĩrwo kĩama! No gũtirĩ kĩama rũkũringĩrwo tiga o kĩama kĩrĩa kĩa mũnabii Jona.
English: But he answered them, “An evil and adulterous generation seeks after a sign, but no sign will be given to it but the sign of Jonah the prophet.

Kikuyu: Nĩ gũkorwo o ta ũrĩa Jona aikarire matukũ matatũ, mũthenya na ũtukũ, arĩ nda ya thamaki ĩrĩa nene, ũguo noguo Mũrũ wa Mũndũ agaikara matukũ matatũ, mũthenya na ũtukũ, arĩ tĩĩri-inĩ.
English: For as Jonah was three days and three nights in the belly of the huge fish, so will the Son of Man be three days and three nights in the heart of the earth.

Kikuyu: Andũ a Nineve nĩmakarũgama hĩndĩ ya ciira hamwe na rũciaro rũrũ marũtuĩre ciira; nĩgũkorwo nĩmeririre rĩrĩa maahunjĩirio nĩ Jona, na rĩu ũmwe mũnene gũkĩra Jona arĩ haha.
English: The men of Nineveh will stand up in the judgment with this generation and will condemn it, for they repented at the preaching of Jonah; and behold, someone greater than Jonah is here.

Kikuyu: Mũthamaki mũndũ-wa-nja wa mwena wa Gũthini nĩakarũgama hĩndĩ ya ciira hamwe na rũciaro rũrũ arũtuĩre ciira; nĩgũkorwo nĩoimire ituri cia thĩ oke athikĩrĩrie ũũgĩ wa Solomoni, na rĩu ũmwe mũnene gũkĩra Solomoni arĩ haha.
English: The Queen of the South will rise up in the judgment with this generation and will condemn it, for she came from the ends of the earth to hear the wisdom of Solomon; and behold, someone greater than Solomon is here.

Kikuyu: “Na rĩrĩ, roho mũũru woima thĩinĩ wa mũndũ, ũtuĩkanagĩria werũ-inĩ ũgĩcaria ũhurũko, na ũkaaga.
English: “When an unclean spirit has gone out of a man, he passes through waterless places seeking rest, and doesn’t find it.  Isaiah 42:1-4  TR adds “of the heart”

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo ũkoiga atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩngũcooka nyũmba ĩrĩa ndoimire.’ Na wacooka-rĩ, ũgakora nyũmba ĩyo ĩrĩ theri, ĩrĩ haate, ĩgathera na ĩgathondekwo wega.
English: Then he says, ‘I will return into my house from which I came;’ and when he has come back, he finds it empty, swept, and put in order.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo roho ũcio ũkagĩĩra maroho mangĩ mũgwanja mooru makĩria maguo mwene, magatoonya magaikara kũu. Naguo mũikarĩre wa mũndũ ũcio ũgathũka gũkĩra wa mbere. Ũguo nĩguo gũkahaana kũrĩ rũciaro rũrũ rwaganu.”
English: Then he goes and takes with himself seven other spirits more evil than he is, and they enter in and dwell there. The last state of that man becomes worse than the first. Even so will it be also to this evil generation.”

Kikuyu: O hĩndĩ ĩyo Jesũ aaragĩria andũ-rĩ, nyina na ariũ a nyina makĩrũgama nja makĩenda kwaria nake.
English: While he was yet speaking to the multitudes, behold, his mother and his brothers stood outside, seeking to speak to him.

Kikuyu: Nake mũndũ ũmwe akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Maitũguo na ariũ a maitũguo marũngiĩ haha nja, makĩenda kwaria nawe.”
English: One said to him, “Behold, your mother and your brothers stand outside, seeking to speak to you.”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmũcookeria akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Maitũ nũũ, na ariũ a maitũ nĩ a?”
English: But he answered him who spoke to him, “Who is my mother? Who are my brothers?”

Kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩorota arutwo ake, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Aya nĩo maitũ na ariũ a maitũ.
English: He stretched out his hand toward his disciples, and said, “Behold, my mother and my brothers!

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mũndũ wothe wĩkaga ũrĩa Baba ũrĩa ũrĩ igũrũ endaga, ũcio nĩwe mũrũ na mwarĩ wa maitũ, na nĩ we maitũ.”
English: For whoever does the will of my Father who is in heaven, he is my brother, and sister, and mother.”

Kikuyu: ũthenya o ro ũcio-rĩ, Jesũ nĩoimire nyũmba agĩthiĩ agĩikara thĩ hũgũrũrũ-inĩ cia iria.
English: n that day Jesus went out of the house and sat by the seaside.

Kikuyu: Ikundi cia andũ aingĩ mũno ikĩũngana harĩa aarĩ nginya ĩgĩtũma atoonye gatarũ na agĩikara thĩ thĩinĩ wayo, nao andũ acio othe makĩrũgama hũgũrũrũ-inĩ cia iria.
English: Great multitudes gathered to him, so that he entered into a boat and sat; and all the multitude stood on the beach.

Kikuyu: Nake akĩmaruta maũndũ maingĩ na ngerekano, akĩmeera atĩrĩ: “Mũrĩmi nĩoimagarire akahure mbeũ ciake.
English: He spoke to them many things in parables, saying, “Behold, a farmer went out to sow.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa aahuraga mbeũ icio-rĩ, imwe ikĩgũa mũkĩra-inĩ wa njĩra, nacio nyoni igĩũka ĩgĩcirĩa.
English: As he sowed, some seeds fell by the roadside, and the birds came and devoured them.

Kikuyu: Ingĩ ikĩgũa rũnyanjara-inĩ kũrĩa gũtaarĩ na tĩĩri mũingĩ. Nacio ikĩmera o narua tondũ tĩĩri ndwarĩ mũriku.
English: Others fell on rocky ground, where they didn’t have much soil, and immediately they sprang up, because they had no depth of earth.

Kikuyu: Na hĩndĩ ĩrĩa riũa rĩarire, rĩgĩcina mĩmera ĩyo, nayo ĩkĩũma tondũ ndĩarĩ na mĩri.
English: When the sun had risen, they were scorched. Because they had no root, they withered away.

Kikuyu: Na mbeũ ingĩ ikĩgũa mĩigua-inĩ, nayo mĩigua ĩgĩkũra, ĩgĩthararia mbeũ icio rĩrĩa ciamerire.
English: Others fell among thorns. The thorns grew up and choked them.

Kikuyu: Ingĩ nacio ikĩgũa tĩĩri-inĩ ũrĩa mũnoru, ikĩgĩa na maciaro imwe ĩgĩciara maita igana, iria ingĩ igĩciara maita mĩrongo ĩtandatũ, na iria ingĩ igĩciara maita mĩrongo ĩtatũ ma iria ciahaandirwo.
English: Others fell on good soil and yielded fruit: some one hundred times as much, some sixty, and some thirty.

Kikuyu: Ũrĩa ũrĩ na matũ ma kũigua, nĩaigue.”
English: He who has ears to hear, let him hear.”

Kikuyu: Nao arutwo ake magĩũka kũrĩ we, makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Waragĩria andũ na ngerekano nĩkĩ?”
English: The disciples came, and said to him, “Why do you speak to them in parables?”

Kikuyu: Nake akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Inyuĩ nĩmũheetwo ũmenyo wa kũmenya hitho cia ũthamaki wa igũrũ, no-omatiheetwo.
English: He answered them, “To you it is given to know the mysteries of the Kingdom of Heaven, but it is not given to them.

Kikuyu: Ũrĩawotheũrĩnaindonĩakoongererwonyingĩaingĩhiemakĩria. Nakeũrĩawothe ũtarĩ, nĩagatuunywo o na kĩrĩa arĩ nakĩo.
English: For whoever has, to him will be given, and he will have abundance; but whoever doesn’t have, from him will be taken away even that which he has.

Kikuyu: Gĩkĩ nĩkĩo gĩtũmaga ndĩmaarĩrie na ngerekano: “Nĩ ũndũ o na gũkorwo nĩmoonaga-rĩ, mationaga; o na gũkorwo nĩmaiguaga-rĩ, matiiguaga kana makamenya.
English: Therefore I speak to them in parables, because seeing they don’t see, and hearing, they don’t hear, neither do they understand.

Kikuyu: Nĩo makinyĩirwo nĩ ũhoro ũrĩa warathirwo nĩ mũnabii Isaia, rĩrĩa oigire atĩrĩ: “ ‘Kũigua mũgũtũũra mũiguaga, no mũtikaamenya ũndũ; kuona mũgũtũũra muonaga, no mũtigakuũkĩrwo.
English: In them the prophecy of Isaiah is fulfilled, which says, ‘By hearing you will hear, and will in no way understand; Seeing you will see, and will in no way perceive;

Kikuyu: “ ‘Nĩgũkorwo ngoro cia andũ aya nĩ ngingitaru; matũ mao matiiguaga, na nĩmahingĩte maitho mao. Tondũ maahota kuona na maitho mao, na maigue na matũ mao, o na ngoro ciao igĩe na ũmenyo, manjookerere, na niĩ ndĩmahonie.’
English: for this people’s heart has grown callous, their ears are dull of hearing, and they have closed their eyes; or else perhaps they might perceive with their eyes, hear with their ears, understand with their heart, and would turn again, and I would heal them.’*

Kikuyu: No kũrathimwo nĩ maitho manyu tondũ nĩmoonaga, na matũ manyu tondũ nĩmaiguaga.
English: “But blessed are your eyes, for they see; and your ears, for they hear.

Kikuyu: Tondũ ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, anabii na andũ aingĩ athingu nĩmerirĩirie kuona maũndũ marĩa mũrona no-o matiamonire, na nĩmerirĩirie kũigua maũndũ marĩa mũraigua no matiamaiguire.
English: For most certainly I tell you that many prophets and righteous men desired to see the things which you see, and didn’t see them; and to hear the things which you hear, and didn’t hear them.

Kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, ta iguai ngĩtaarĩria ũhoro wa ngerekano ĩyo ya mũhuri wa mbeũ:
English: “Hear, then, the parable of the farmer.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa mũndũ o wothe aigua ndũmĩrĩri ya ũthamaki na akaaga kũmĩmenya-rĩ, hĩndĩ ĩyo ũrĩa mũũru okaga akamũtunya kĩrĩa kĩhaande ngoro-inĩ yake. Ĩno nĩyo mbeũ ĩrĩa yahurirwo mũkĩra-inĩ wa njĩra.
English: When anyone hears the word of the Kingdom and doesn’t understand it, the evil one comes and snatches away that which has been sown in his heart. This is what was sown by the roadside.

Kikuyu: Ũrĩa wamũkĩrire mbeũ ĩrĩa yagũire rũnyanjara-inĩ nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa ũiguaga kiugo, na o rĩmwe agakĩamũkĩra arĩ na gĩkeno.
English: What was sown on the rocky places, this is he who hears the word and immediately with joy receives it;

Kikuyu: No tondũ ndarĩ mĩri-rĩ, aikaraga o kahinda kanini. Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa thĩĩna woka, kana kwagĩa na kĩnyariirano nĩ ũndũ wa kiugo-rĩ, agagĩtiganĩria o narua.
English: yet he has no root in himself, but endures for a while. When oppression or persecution arises because of the word, immediately he stumbles.  Isaiah 6:9-10

Kikuyu: Nake ũrĩa wamũkagĩra mbeũ ĩrĩa yagũire mĩigua-inĩ-rĩ, nĩ mũndũ ũrĩa ũiguaga kiugo, no mĩhangʼo ya mũtũũrĩre ũyũ, na kũheenererio nĩ ũtonga, igagĩthararia, ikagiria kĩgĩe na maciaro.
English: What was sown among the thorns, this is he who hears the word, but the cares of this age and the deceitfulness of riches choke the word, and he becomes unfruitful.

Kikuyu: No ũrĩa wamũkĩrire mbeũ ĩrĩa yagũiretĩĩri-inĩũrĩamũnoru-rĩ, nĩmũndũũrĩaũiguagakiugo, naagakĩmenya. Nakeagaciaramaciaro, ũmwe akaruta maita igana, ũngĩ maita mĩrongo ĩtandatũ, na ũngĩ maita mĩrongo ĩtatũ ma mbeũ ĩrĩa yahaandĩtwo.”
English: What was sown on the good ground, this is he who hears the word and understands it, who most certainly bears fruit and produces, some one hundred times as much, some sixty, and some thirty.”

Kikuyu: Ningĩ Jesũ akĩmahe ngerekano ĩngĩ, akĩmeera atĩrĩ: “Ũthamaki wa igũrũ ũhaana ta mũndũ ũrĩa wahaandire mbeũ njega mũgũnda wake.
English: He set another parable before them, saying, “The Kingdom of Heaven is like a man who sowed good seed in his field,

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩa andũ othe maakomete-rĩ, thũ yake ĩgĩũka, ĩkĩhura itindiĩ thĩinĩ wa ngano, na ĩgĩĩthiĩra.
English: but while people slept, his enemy came and sowed darnel weeds† also among the wheat, and went away.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa ngano yamerire na igĩciara-rĩ, hĩndĩ ĩyo itindiĩ o narĩo rĩkĩonekana.
English: But when the blade sprang up and produced grain, then the darnel weeds appeared also.

Kikuyu: “Ndungata cia mwene mũgũnda igĩũka kũrĩ we, ikĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Mwathi witũ, githĩ ndwahaandire mbeũ njega mũgũnda waku? Kaĩ itindiĩ rĩkiumĩte kũ?’
English: The servants of the householder came and said to him, ‘Sir, didn’t you sow good seed in your field? Where did these darnel weeds come from?’

Kikuyu: “Agĩcookia atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩ mũndũ ũrĩ ũthũ na niĩ wekire ũguo.’ “Ndungata icio ikĩmũũria atĩrĩ, ‘Nĩũkwenda tũthiĩ, tũkarĩmunye?’
English: “He said to them, ‘An enemy has done this.’ “The servants asked him, ‘Do you want us to go and gather them up?’

Kikuyu: “Nake agĩcookia atĩrĩ, ‘Aca, tondũ mũkĩmunya itindiĩ rĩu, mwahota kũrĩmunyanĩria na ngano.
English: “But he said, ‘No, lest perhaps while you gather up the darnel weeds, you root up the wheat with them.

Kikuyu: Rekei ikũranĩre nginya hĩndĩ ya magetha. Hĩndĩ ĩyo-rĩ, nĩngeera agethi atĩrĩ: Ambai mũmunye itindiĩ na mũrĩohe itĩĩa, nĩguo rĩcinwo; mũcooke mũcookanĩrĩrie ngano, mũmĩige ikũmbĩ rĩakwa.’ ” Ngerekano ya Mbeũ ya Karatarĩ, na ya Ndawa ya Kũimbia Mĩgate
English: Let both grow together until the harvest, and in the harvest time I will tell the reapers, “First, gather up the darnel weeds, and bind them in bundles to burn them; but gather the wheat into my barn.” ’ ”

Kikuyu: Nĩamaheire ngerekano ĩngĩ, akĩmeera atĩrĩ: “Ũthamaki wa igũrũ ũtariĩ ta mbeũ ya karatarĩ, ĩrĩa mũndũ oire akĩhaanda mũgũnda wake.
English: He set another parable before them, saying, “The Kingdom of Heaven is like a grain of mustard seed which a man took, and sowed in his field,

Kikuyu: O na akorwo mbeũ ĩyo nĩyo nini mũno kũrĩ mbeũ ciothe-rĩ, rĩrĩa yakũra, nĩĩnenehaga kũrĩ mĩmera ĩrĩa ĩngĩ ya mũgũnda na ĩgatuĩka mũtĩ, nacio nyoni cia rĩera-inĩ igooka igaaka itara honge-inĩ ciaguo.”
English: which indeed is smaller than all seeds. But when it is grown, it is greater than the herbs and becomes a tree, so that the birds of the air come and lodge in its branches.”

Kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩmahe ngerekano o ĩngĩ, akĩmeera atĩrĩ: “Ũthamaki wa igũrũ ũhaana ta ndawa ya kũimbia mĩgate ĩrĩa mũndũ-wa-nja oire na akĩmĩtukania na mũtu mũingĩ o nginya mũtu ũcio wothe ũkĩimbio nĩyo.”
English: He spoke another parable to them. “The Kingdom of Heaven is like yeast which a woman took and hid in three measures‡ of meal, until it was all leavened.”

Kikuyu: Jesũ aaragĩria kĩrĩndĩ maũndũ macio mothe na ngerekano na ndaamaragĩria ũndũ o na ũmwe atekũhũthĩra ngerekano.
English: Jesus spoke all these things in parables to the multitudes; and without a parable, he didn’t speak to them,

Kikuyu: Nĩguo ũhoro ũrĩa waaririo na kanua ka mũnabii ũhinge rĩrĩa oigire atĩrĩ: “Ngaatumũra kanua gakwa njarie na ngerekano, nĩngaaria maũndũ marĩa matũire marĩ mahithe kuuma rĩrĩa thĩ yombirwo.” Gũtaarĩria Ngerekano ya Itindiĩ
English: that it might be fulfilled which was spoken through the prophet, saying, “I will open my mouth in parables; I will utter things hidden from the foundation of the world.”*

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo agĩtiga kĩrĩndĩ, agĩtoonya nyũmba. Arutwo ake magĩũka kũrĩ we, makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Tũtaarĩrie ngerekano ya itindiĩ rĩrĩa rĩarĩ thĩinĩ wa mũgũnda.”
English: Then Jesus sent the multitudes away, and went into the house. His disciples came to him, saying, “Explain to us the parable of the darnel weeds of the field.”

Kikuyu: Nake akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Ũrĩa wahaandire mbeũ njega nĩ Mũrũ wa Mũndũ.
English: He answered them, “He who sows the good seed is the Son of Man,

Kikuyu: Mũgũnda nĩ thĩ, nayo mbeũ njega nĩcio ciana cia ũthamaki. Itindiĩ nĩ ciana cia ũrĩa mũũru,
English: the field is the world, the good seeds are the children of the Kingdom, and the darnel weeds are the children of the evil one.

Kikuyu: nayo thũ ĩrĩa ĩrĩhaandaga nĩ ũrĩa mũũru. Magetha nĩ ithirĩro rĩa mahinda maya, na agethi nĩ araika.
English: The enemy who sowed them is the devil. The harvest is the end of the age, and the reapers are angels.

Kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio o ta ũrĩa itindiĩ rĩmunyagwo rĩgacinwo na mwaki-rĩ, ũguo noguo gũkahaana hĩndĩ ya ithirĩro rĩa mahinda maya.
English: As therefore the darnel weeds are gathered up and burned with fire; so will it be at the end of this age.

Kikuyu: Mũrũ wa Mũndũ nĩagatũma araika ake, na nĩmakeheria maũndũ mothe marĩa marehaga mehia na andũ arĩa othe mekaga ũũru ũthamaki-inĩ wake.
English: The Son of Man will send out his angels, and they will gather out of his Kingdom all things that cause stumbling and those who do iniquity,

Kikuyu: Nĩmakamaikia thĩinĩ wa icua rĩa mwaki, kũrĩa gũgaakorwo kĩrĩro na kũhagarania magego.
English: and will cast them into the furnace of fire. There will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo arĩa athingu magaakenga ta riũa thĩinĩ wa ũthamaki wa Ithe wao. Ũrĩa ũrĩ na matũ nĩ aigue. Ngerekano ya Mũthiithũ Mũhithe, na Ruru
English: Then the righteous will shine like the sun in the Kingdom of their Father. He who has ears to hear, let him hear.

Kikuyu: “Ũthamaki wa igũrũ ũhaanaine na mũthiithũ wahithĩtwo mũgũnda. Na rĩrĩa mũndũ aawoonire, akĩũhitha rĩngĩ, na nĩ ũndũ wa ũrĩa akenete, agĩthiĩ akĩendia indo ciothe iria arĩ nacio akĩgũra mũgũnda ũcio.
English: “Again, the Kingdom of Heaven is like treasure hidden in the field, which a man found and hid. In his joy, he goes and sells all that he has and buys that field.

Kikuyu: “Ningĩ ũthamaki wa igũrũ ũhaanaine na mwonjorithia wacaragia ruru njega.
English: “Again, the Kingdom of Heaven is like a man who is a merchant seeking fine pearls,

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa oonire ĩmwe ya thogora mũnene-rĩ, agĩthiĩ akĩendia indo ciake ciothe iria aarĩ nacio, akĩmĩgũra. Ngerekano ya Neti
English: who having found one pearl of great price, he went and sold all that he had and bought it.  darnel is a weed grass (probably bearded darnel or lolium temulentum) that looks very much like wheat until it is mature, when the difference becomes very apparent.  literally, three sata. Three sata is about 39

Kikuyu: “O rĩngĩ, ũthamaki wa igũrũ ũhaanaine na neti ĩrĩa yaikirio iria-inĩ, nayo ĩgĩtega thamaki cia mĩthemba yothe.
English: “Again, the Kingdom of Heaven is like a dragnet that was cast into the sea and gathered some fish of every kind,

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa yaiyũrire, ategi acio a thamaki makĩmĩguucia hũgũrũrũ-inĩ cia iria. Hĩndĩ ĩyo magĩikara thĩ, magĩthuura thamaki iria njega, magĩciĩkĩra ikabũ-inĩ, no iria njũru magĩcite.
English: which, when it was filled, fishermen drew up on the beach. They sat down and gathered the good into containers, but the bad they threw away.

Kikuyu: Ũguo nĩguo gũgaakorwo hĩndĩ ya ithirĩro rĩa mahinda maya. Araika nĩmagooka maamũranie arĩa aaganu kuuma kũrĩ arĩa athingu,
English: So it will be in the end of the world.§ The angels will come and separate the wicked from among the righteous,

Kikuyu: na maikie arĩa aaganu icua-inĩ rĩa mwaki, kũrĩa gũgaakorwo na kĩrĩro na kũhagarania magego.”
English: and will cast them into the furnace of fire. There will be weeping and gnashing of teeth.”

Kikuyu: Jesũ akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Nĩmwataũkĩrwo nĩ maũndũ macio mothe?” Magĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Ĩĩ, nĩtwataũkĩrwo.”
English: Jesus said to them, “Have you understood all these things?” They answered him, “Yes, Lord.”

Kikuyu: Akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, mũrutani wa watho o wothe ũrĩa ũrutĩtwo ũhoro wa ũthamaki wa igũrũ-rĩ, ahaanaine na mwene nyũmba ũrĩa ũrutaga mũthiithũ-inĩ wake indo iria njerũ o na iria ngũrũ.” Mũnabii ũtarĩ Mũtĩĩe
English: He said to them, “Therefore every scribe who has been made a disciple in the Kingdom of Heaven is like a man who is a householder, who brings out of his treasure new and old things.”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ aarĩkia kũheana ngerekano icio-rĩ, akiuma kũu.
English: When Jesus had finished these parables, he departed from there.

Kikuyu: Aakinya itũũra rĩa kwao-rĩ, akĩambĩrĩria kũrutana thunagogi-inĩ ciao, nao makĩgega. Makĩũrania atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ ũyũ arutĩte kũ ũũgĩ ũyũ na ciĩko ici cia hinya?
English: Coming into his own country, he taught them in their synagogue, so that they were astonished and said, “Where did this man get this wisdom and these mighty works?

Kikuyu: Githĩ ũyũ ti we mũrũ wa mũtharamara? Githĩ rĩĩtwa rĩa nyina ti Mariamu, na githĩ ariũ a nyina ti Jakubu, na Jusufu, na Simoni, na Juda?
English: Isn’t this the carpenter’s son? Isn’t his mother called Mary, and his brothers James, Joses, Simon, and Judas?†

Kikuyu: Na githĩ aarĩ a nyina othe tũtirĩ nao? Mũndũ ũyũ akĩrutĩte maũndũ maya mothe kũ?”
English: Aren’t all of his sisters with us? Where then did this man get all of these things?”

Kikuyu: Nao makĩmũrega. No Jesũ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Kũndũ mũnabii ataheagwo gĩtĩĩo no itũũra-inĩ rĩake na gwake mũciĩ.”
English: They were offended by him. But Jesus said to them, “A prophet is not without honor, except in his own country and in his own house.”

Kikuyu: Nake ndaaringire ciama nyingĩ kũu, tondũ wa ũrĩa maagĩte gwĩtĩkia.
English: He didn’t do many mighty works there because of their unbelief.

Kikuyu: a rĩrĩ, hĩndĩ ĩyo, Mũthamaki Herode nĩaiguire ngumo ya Jesũ,
English: t that time, Herod the tetrarch heard the report concerning Jesus,

Kikuyu: nake akĩĩra arĩa maamũtungataga atĩrĩ, “Ũcio nĩ Johana Mũbatithania; nĩ ariũkĩte kuuma kũrĩ arĩa akuũ! Nĩkĩo arĩ na hinya mũnene ũguo wa kũringa ciama.”
English: and said to his servants, “This is John the Baptizer. He is risen from the dead. That is why these powers work in him.”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Herode nĩanyiitĩte Johana akamuoha, na akamũikia njeera nĩ ũndũ wa Herodia, ũrĩa warĩ mũtumia wa mũrũ wa nyina Filipu,
English: For Herod had arrested John, bound him, and put him in prison for the sake of Herodias, his brother Philip’s wife.

Kikuyu: tondũ Johana nĩamwĩrĩte atĩrĩ: “Watho ndũgwĩtĩkĩrĩtie ũmũhikie.”
English: For John said to him, “It is not lawful for you to have her.”

Kikuyu: Herode nĩendaga kũũraga Johana, no nĩetigagĩra andũ, tondũ nĩmeetĩkĩtie atĩ Johana aarĩ mũnabii.
English: When he would have put him to death, he feared the multitude, because they counted him as a prophet.

Kikuyu: Mũthenya wa gũkũngũĩra gũciarwo kwa Herode-rĩ, mwarĩ wa Herodia nĩainĩire andũ, na agĩkenia Herode mũno.
English: But when Herod’s birthday came, the daughter of Herodias danced among them and pleased Herod.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio Herode akĩĩhĩta na mwĩhĩtwa atĩ nĩekũmũhe kĩrĩa gĩothe angĩendire.
English: Therefore he promised with an oath to give her whatever she should ask.

Kikuyu: Nake mũirĩtu ũcio aarĩkia gũtaarwo nĩ nyina, akiuga atĩrĩ, “He mũtwe wa Johana Mũbatithania o rĩu ũrĩ thĩinĩ wa kiuga.”
English: She, being prompted by her mother, said, “Give me here on a platter the head of John the Baptizer.”

Kikuyu: Mũthamaki nĩataangĩkire mũno, no nĩ ũndũ wa mwĩhĩtwa wake, na ageni arĩa maarĩ iruga-inĩ rĩake-rĩ, agĩathana atĩ mũirĩtu ũcio aheo kĩrĩa eetĩtie.
English: The king was grieved, but for the sake of his oaths and of those who sat at the table with him, he commanded it to be given,

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio agĩtũmana kũu njeera na Johana akĩrengwo mũtwe.
English: and he sent and beheaded John in the prison.

Kikuyu: Naguo mũtwe ũcio wake ũkĩrehwo na kiuga, ũkĩnengerwo mũirĩtu ũcio, nake akĩũtwarĩra nyina.
English: His head was brought on a platter and given to the young lady; and she brought it to her mother.

Kikuyu: Nao arutwo a Johana magĩthiĩ makĩoya kĩimba gĩake, magĩgĩthika, magĩcooka magĩthiĩ makĩĩra Jesũ. Jesũ Kũhũũnia Andũ Ngiri Ithano
English: His disciples came, took the body, and buried it. Then they went and told Jesus.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa Jesũ aiguire ũhoro ũcio, akiuma kũu ahaicĩte gatarũ andũ matekũmenya, agĩthiĩ handũ hataarĩ na andũ. Nakĩo kĩrĩndĩ kĩaigua ũguo-rĩ, gĩkiuma matũũra-inĩ gĩkĩmũrũmĩrĩra na magũrũ.
English: Now when Jesus heard this, he withdrew from there in a boat to a deserted place apart. When the multitudes heard it, they followed him on foot from the cities.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa Jesũ oimire gatarũ na akĩona andũ aingĩ, akĩmaiguĩra tha, na akĩhonia arũaru ao.
English: Jesus went out, and he saw a great multitude. He had compassion on them and healed their sick.

Kikuyu: Na gwakuhĩrĩria gũtuka-rĩ, arutwo ake magĩthiĩ harĩ we, makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Gũkũ nĩ kũndũ gũtarĩ mĩciĩ, na nĩkũratuka. Ĩra andũ aya mathiĩ, nĩgeetha makegũrĩre irio tũtũũra-inĩ.”
English: When evening had come, his disciples came to him, saying, “This place is deserted, and the hour is already late. Send the multitudes away, that they may go into the villages, and buy themselves food.”

Kikuyu: Jesũ akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Hatirĩ bata wa mathiĩ. Mahei gĩa kũrĩa inyuĩ ene.”
English: But Jesus said to them, “They don’t need to go away. You give them something to eat.”

Kikuyu: Nao makĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Haha tũrĩ na mĩgate ĩtano tu na thamaki igĩrĩ.”
English: They told him, “We only have here five loaves and two fish.”

Kikuyu: Akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ndeherai haha.”
English: He said, “Bring them here to me.”

Kikuyu: Nake agĩathana andũ maikare thĩ nyeki-inĩ. Akĩoya mĩgate ĩyo ĩtano na thamaki icio igĩrĩ, akĩrora igũrũ, agĩcookia ngaatho, akĩenyũranga mĩgate ĩyo. Agĩcooka akĩmĩnengera arutwo, nao arutwo makĩhe andũ.
English: He commanded the multitudes to sit down on the grass; and he took the five loaves and the two fish, and looking up to heaven, he blessed, broke and gave the loaves to the disciples; and the disciples gave to the multitudes.  or, end of the age.  or, Judah

Kikuyu: Othe makĩrĩa makĩhũũna, nao arutwo makĩũngania cienyũ iria ciatigarĩte makĩiyũria ciondo ikũmi na igĩrĩ.
English: They all ate and were filled. They took up twelve baskets full of that which remained left over from the broken pieces.

Kikuyu: Mũigana wa andũ arĩa maarĩire maarĩ ta arũme ngiri ithano, andũ-a-nja na ciana matatarĩtwo. Jesũ Kũgerera Maaĩ Igũrũ
English: Those who ate were about five thousand men, in addition to women and children.

Kikuyu: O hĩndĩ ĩyo Jesũ akĩĩra arutwo ake matoonye gatarũ, mathiĩ mbere yake mũrĩmo ũrĩa ũngĩ wa iria mũira wa oigĩre kĩrĩndĩ ũhoro.
English: Immediately Jesus made the disciples get into the boat and go ahead of him to the other side, while he sent the multitudes away.

Kikuyu: Thuutha wa kũmoigĩra ũhoro-rĩ, akĩambata kĩrĩma-inĩ arĩ wiki nĩguo akahooe. Na gũgĩtuka aarĩ o ho wiki,
English: After he had sent the multitudes away, he went up into the mountain by himself to pray. When evening had come, he was there alone.

Kikuyu: no gatarũ nĩkarĩkĩtie gũthiĩ itĩĩna iraiharaihu kuuma thĩ nyũmũ, gakĩhũũragwo nĩ makũmbĩ ma maaĩ tondũ kerekeire na kũrĩa rũhuho ruoimaga.
English: But the boat was now in the middle of the sea, distressed by the waves, for the wind was contrary.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, ta thaa kenda cia ũtukũ-rĩ, Jesũ agĩthiĩ kũrĩ o agereire maaĩ igũrũ.
English: In the fourth watch of the night,† Jesus came to them, walking on the sea.*

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa arutwo maamuonire agĩthiĩ agereire maaĩ igũrũ, makĩnyiitwo nĩ guoya, makiuga atĩrĩ, “Ĩrĩa nĩ ngoma,” na magĩkaya nĩ gwĩtigĩra.
English: When the disciples saw him walking on the sea, they were troubled, saying, “It’s a ghost!” and they cried out for fear.

Kikuyu: No hĩndĩ o ĩyo Jesũ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Ũmĩrĩriai! Nĩ niĩ. Tigai gwĩtigĩra.”
English: But immediately Jesus spoke to them, saying, “Cheer up! It is I! ‡ Don’t be afraid.”

Kikuyu: Petero akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Mwathani, akorwo nĩwe-rĩ, njĩĩra njũke kũrĩ we ngereire maaĩ igũrũ.”
English: Peter answered him and said, “Lord, if it is you, command me to come to you on the waters.”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Ũka.” Hĩndĩ ĩyo, Petero akiuma gatarũ, agĩthiĩ agereire maaĩ igũrũ erekeire harĩ Jesũ.
English: He said, “Come!” Peter stepped down from the boat and walked on the waters to come to Jesus.

Kikuyu: No rĩrĩa oonire rũhuho, agĩĩtigĩra, na akĩambĩrĩria gũtoonyerera maaĩ-inĩ, agĩkaya, akiuga atĩrĩ, “Mwathani, honokia!”
English: But when he saw that the wind was strong, he was afraid, and beginning to sink, he cried out, saying, “Lord, save me!”

Kikuyu: O rĩmwe, Jesũ agĩtambũrũkia guoko, akĩmũnyiita, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Wee kaĩ ũrĩ na wĩtĩkio mũnini- ĩ! Wagĩa na nganja nĩkĩ?”
English: Immediately Jesus stretched out his hand, took hold of him, and said to him, “You of little faith, why did you doubt?”

Kikuyu: Na hĩndĩ ĩrĩa maahaicire gatarũ-rĩ, rũhuho rũgĩtiga kũhurutana.
English: When they got up into the boat, the wind ceased.

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo arĩa maarĩ thĩinĩ wa gatarũ makĩmũgooca, makiuga atĩrĩ, “Nĩ ma wee nĩwe Mũrũ wa Ngai.”
English: Those who were in the boat came and worshiped him, saying, “You are truly the Son of God!”

Kikuyu: Na maarĩkia kũringa mũrĩmo ũrĩa ũngĩ, magĩkinya bũrũri wa Genesareti.
English: When they had crossed over, they came to the land of Gennesaret.

Kikuyu: Na hĩndĩ ĩrĩa andũ a kũu maamenyire atĩ nĩ Jesũ, makĩhunjia ũhoro ngʼongo ciothe iria ciamarigiicĩirie. Nao makĩmũrehera andũ othe arĩa maarĩ arũaru,
English: When the people of that place recognized him, they sent into all that surrounding region and brought to him all who were sick;

Kikuyu: na makĩmũthaitha areke arũaru mahutie gĩcũrĩ kĩa nguo yake, na arĩa othe maamũhutirie makĩhonio.
English: and they begged him that they might just touch the fringe§ of his garment. As many as touched it were made whole.

Kikuyu: a rĩrĩ, thuutha ũcio Afarisai amwe na arutani a watho magĩũka kũrĩ Jesũ moimĩte Jerusalemu, makĩmũũria atĩrĩ,
English: hen Pharisees and scribes came to Jesus from Jerusalem, saying,

Kikuyu: “Nĩ kĩĩ gĩtũmaga arutwo aku mathũkie kĩrĩra gĩa athuuri? Matiĩthambaga moko rĩrĩa mekũrĩa irio!”
English: “Why do your disciples disobey the tradition of the elders? For they don’t wash their hands when they eat bread.”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Na inyuĩ mũthũkagia watho wa Ngai nĩ ũndũ wa irĩra cianyu nĩkĩ?
English: He answered them, “Why do you also disobey the commandment of God because of your tradition?

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Ngai oigire atĩrĩ, ‘Tĩĩa thoguo na nyũkwa’ na ‘Mũndũ wa kũruma ithe kana nyina no nginya ooragwo.’
English: For God commanded, ‘Honor your father and your mother,’* and, ‘He who speaks evil of father or mother, let him be put to death.’*

Kikuyu: No inyuĩ muugaga atĩ mũndũ angĩĩra ithe kana nyina atĩrĩ, ‘Ũteithio ũrĩa ingĩagũteithia naguo, nĩ kĩheo kĩamũrĩre Ngai,’
English: But you say, ‘Whoever may tell his father or his mother, “Whatever help you might otherwise have gotten from me is a gift devoted to God,”

Kikuyu: mũndũ ũcio ndarĩ bata wa ‘gũtĩĩa ithe’ naguo. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio mũgagĩtua kiugo kĩa Ngai gĩa tũhũ nĩ ũndũ wa kĩrĩra kĩanyu.
English: he shall not honor his father or mother.’ You have made the commandment of God void because of your tradition.

Kikuyu: Inyuĩ hinga ici! Isaia nĩamenyete wega hĩndĩ ĩrĩa aarathire ũhoro wanyu, akiuga atĩrĩ:
English: You hypocrites! Well did Isaiah prophesy of you, saying,

Kikuyu: “ ‘Andũ aya maaheaga gĩtĩĩo na mĩromo, no ngoro ciao irĩ kũraya na niĩ.
English: ‘These people draw near to me with their mouth, and honor me with their lips; but their heart is far from me.

Kikuyu: Nao maahooyaga o tũhũ; morutani marĩa marutanaga no mawatho ma andũ.’ ”
English: And they worship me in vain, teaching as doctrine rules made by men.’ ”*

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ agĩĩta kĩrĩndĩ kĩu gĩũke harĩ we, agĩkĩĩra atĩrĩ, “Thikĩrĩriai na mũtaũkĩrwo.
English: He summoned the multitude, and said to them, “Hear, and understand.

Kikuyu: Kĩrĩa gĩtoonyaga na kanua ka mũndũ ti kĩo ‘kĩmũthaahagia’, no kĩrĩa kiumaga kanua gake nĩkĩo ‘kĩmũthaa- hagia.’ ”
English: That which enters into the mouth doesn’t defile the man; but that which proceeds out of the mouth, this defiles the man.”

Kikuyu: Na hĩndĩ ĩyo arutwo ake magĩthiĩ harĩ we, makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Nĩũĩ atĩ Afarisai nĩmararakarire rĩrĩa maraiguire ũhoro ũcio?”
English: Then the disciples came and said to him, “Do you know that the Pharisees were offended when they heard this saying?”

Kikuyu: Nake akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Mũmera o wothe ũtaahandĩtwo nĩ Baba ũrĩa ũrĩ igũrũ nĩũkamunywo na mĩri.
English: But he answered, “Every plant which my heavenly Father didn’t plant will be uprooted.  The night was equally divided into four watches, so the fourth watch is approximately 3:00 a.m. to sunrise.  See Job 9:8  or, I AM!  or, tassel  Exodus 20:12; Deuteronomy 5:16

Kikuyu: Tiganai nao; acio nĩ atongoria atumumu. Mũtumumu angĩtongoria mũtumumu ũngĩ, eerĩ no kũgũa mangĩgũa irima.”
English: Leave them alone. They are blind guides of the blind. If the blind guide the blind, both will fall into a pit.”

Kikuyu: Nake Petero akiuga atĩrĩ, “Tũtaũrĩre ngerekano ĩyo.”
English: Peter answered him, “Explain the parable to us.”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “O na inyuĩ mũtirĩ mũrataũkĩrwo o na rĩu?
English: So Jesus said, “Do you also still not understand?

Kikuyu: Kaĩ mũtooĩ atĩ kĩndũ kĩrĩa gĩtoonyaga na kanua gĩthiiaga nda, gĩgacooka gĩkoima mwĩrĩ?
English: Don’t you understand that whatever goes into the mouth passes into the belly and then out of the body?

Kikuyu: No maũndũ marĩa moimaga kanua moimaga ngoro-inĩ, na nĩmo ‘mathaahagia’ mũndũ.
English: But the things which proceed out of the mouth come out of the heart, and they defile the man.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ngoro-inĩ nĩkuo kuumaga meciiria mooru, na ũragani, na ũtharia, na ũhũũri maraya, na ũici, na kũiganĩrĩra igenyo, na njambanio.
English: For out of the heart come evil thoughts, murders, adulteries, sexual sins, thefts, false testimony, and blasphemies.

Kikuyu: Macio nĩmo maũndũ marĩa ‘mathaahagia’ mũndũ; no kũrĩa irio na moko matarĩ mathambie matingĩtũma mũndũ agĩe na ‘thaahu’.”
English: These are the things which defile the man; but to eat with unwashed hands doesn’t defile the man.”

Kikuyu: Ningĩ Jesũ akiuma kũu, agĩthiĩ ndeere-inĩ cia Turo na Sidoni.
English: Jesus went out from there and withdrew into the region of Tyre and Sidon.

Kikuyu: Mũndũ-wa-nja Mũkaanani woimĩte gũkuhĩ na kũu agĩũka kũrĩ we, akĩmũkayagĩra, akiugaga atĩrĩ, “Mwathani, Mũrũ wa Daudi, njiguĩra tha! Mũirĩtu wakwa nĩarathĩĩnio mũno nĩ ndaimono.”
English: Behold, a Canaanite woman came out from those borders and cried, saying, “Have mercy on me, Lord, you son of David! My daughter is severely possessed by a demon!”

Kikuyu: No Jesũ ndarĩ ũndũ o na ũmwe oigire. Nĩ ũndũ ũcio arutwo ake magĩthiĩ kũrĩ we makĩmũringĩrĩria, makĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Mwĩre athiĩ, tondũ nĩaratũrũmĩrĩra agĩkayaga.”
English: But he answered her not a word. His disciples came and begged him, saying, “Send her away; for she cries after us.”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Ndaatũmirwo o kũrĩ ngʼondu cia nyũmba ya Isiraeli iria ciũrĩte tu.”
English: But he answered, “I wasn’t sent to anyone but the lost sheep of the house of Israel.”

Kikuyu: Nake mũndũ-wa-nja ũcio agĩũka, akĩmũturĩria ndu, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Mwathani, ndeithia!”
English: But she came and worshiped him, saying, “Lord, help me.”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ agĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Gũtiagĩrĩire kuoya mũgate wa ciana ũikĩrie ngui.”*
English: But he answered, “It is not appropriate to take the children’s bread and throw it to the dogs.”

Kikuyu: Nake mũndũ-wa-nja ũcio akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ĩĩ nĩguo Mwathani; no o na ngui nĩirĩĩaga rũitĩki rũrĩa rũgũaga kuuma metha-inĩ ya mwene cio.”
English: But she said, “Yes, Lord, but even the dogs eat the crumbs which fall from their masters’ table.”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Mũtumia ũyũ, wĩtĩkio waku nĩ mũnene! Ihooya rĩaku nĩrĩahingio.” Nake mwarĩ akĩhonio ithaa o rĩu. Jesũ kũhe Andũ Ngiri Inya Irio
English: Then Jesus answered her, “Woman, great is your faith! Be it done to you even as you desire.” And her daughter was healed from that hour.

Kikuyu: Jesũ akiuma kũu, agĩthiĩ agereire hũgũrũrũ-inĩ cia iria rĩa Galili, agĩcooka akĩambata kĩrĩma- inĩ, agĩikara thĩ.
English: Jesus departed from there and came near to the sea of Galilee; and he went up on the mountain and sat there.

Kikuyu: Nakĩo kĩrĩndĩ kĩnene gĩgĩũka kũrĩ we, gĩkĩmũrehera andũ arĩa maathuaga, na atumumu, na arĩa maarĩ onju na arĩa mataaragia, na angĩ aingĩ, makĩmaiga magũrũ-inĩ make; nake akĩmahonia.
English: Great multitudes came to him, having with them the lame, blind, mute, maimed, and many others, and they put them down at his feet. He healed them,

Kikuyu: Nao andũ makĩgega rĩrĩa moonire arĩa mataaragia makĩaria, na arĩa onju mahonetio, na arĩa maathuaga magĩĩtwara, na atumumu makĩona. Nao makĩgooca Ngai wa Isiraeli.
English: so that the multitude wondered when they saw the mute speaking, the injured healed, the lame walking, and the blind seeing—and they glorified the God of Israel.

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ agĩĩta arutwo ake moke harĩ we, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Nĩndĩraiguĩra andũ aya tha tondũ makoretwo hamwe na niĩ mĩthenya ĩtatũ, na matirĩ na kĩndũ gĩa kũrĩa. Ndikwenda mathiĩ marĩ ahũtu, tondũ maahota kũringĩkĩra njĩra.”
English: Jesus summoned his disciples and said, “I have compassion on the multitude, because they have continued with me now three days and have nothing to eat. I don’t want to send them away fasting, or they might faint on the way.”

Kikuyu: Nao arutwo ake magĩcookia atĩrĩ, “Tũngĩruta kũ mĩgate ya kũhũũnia kĩrĩndĩ ta gĩkĩ, gũkũ werũ- inĩ?”
English: The disciples said to him, “Where could we get so many loaves in a deserted place as to satisfy so great a multitude?”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmooria, atĩrĩ, “Mũrĩ na mĩgate ĩigana?” Nao makĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Tũrĩ na mĩgate mũgwanja, na tũthamaki tũnini tũtarĩ tũingĩ.”
English: Jesus said to them, “How many loaves do you have?” They said, “Seven, and a few small fish.”

Kikuyu: Nake akĩĩra kĩrĩndĩ kĩu gĩikare thĩ.
English: He commanded the multitude to sit down on the ground;

Kikuyu: Agĩcooka akĩoya mĩgate ĩyo mũgwanja na tũthamaki tũu, na aarĩkia gũcookia ngaatho-rĩ, akĩmĩenyũranga, akĩmĩnengera arutwo nao makĩhe andũ.
English: and he took the seven loaves and the fish. He gave thanks and broke them, and gave to the disciples, and the disciples to the multitudes.

Kikuyu: Othe makĩrĩa makĩhũũna. Thuutha ũcio arutwo makĩũngania cienyũ iria ciatigarĩte makĩiyũria ciondo mũgwanja.
English: They all ate and were filled. They took up seven baskets full of the broken pieces that were left over.

Kikuyu: Mũigana wa andũ arĩa maarĩire maarĩ andũ ngiri inya, andũ-a-nja na ciana matatarĩtwo.
English: Those who ate were four thousand men, in addition to women and children.

Kikuyu: Jesũ aarĩkia kuugĩra kĩrĩndĩ ũhoro, agĩtoonya gatarũ, agĩthiĩ mwena wa Magadani.
English: Then he sent away the multitudes, got into the boat, and came into the borders of Magdala.

Kikuyu: a rĩrĩ, Afarisai na Asadukai magĩũka kũrĩ Jesũ, makĩmũgeria na kũmwĩra amaringĩre kĩama kiumĩte igũrũ makĩone.
English: he Pharisees and Sadducees came, and testing him, asked him to show them a sign from heaven.

Kikuyu: Nake akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa gwatuka, muugaga atĩrĩ, ‘Kaĩ nĩgũkaara-ĩ, tondũ igũrũ nĩ gũtune’.
English: But he answered them, “When it is evening, you say, ‘It will be fair weather, for the sky is red.’

Kikuyu: Ningĩ rũciinĩ muugaga atĩrĩ, ‘Ũmũthĩ nĩgũkũgĩa kĩhuhũkanio, tondũ igũrũ nĩ gũtune na gũgathimba.’ Nĩmũũĩ gũtaũra ũrĩa igũrũ rĩtariĩ, no mũtingĩhota gũtaũra imenyithia cia mahinda maya tũrĩ.
English: In the morning, ‘It will be foul weather today, for the sky is red and threatening.’ Hypocrites! You know how to discern the appearance of the sky, but you can’t discern the signs of the times!

Kikuyu: Rũciaro rũrũ rwaganu na rwa ũtharia rũũragia rũringĩrwo kĩama, no gũtirĩ kĩama rũkũringĩrwo tiga kĩama kĩrĩa kĩa Jona.” Nake Jesũ akĩmatiga, agĩĩthiĩra. Ndawa ya Kũimbia Mĩgate ya Afarisai na Asadukai
English: An evil and adulterous generation seeks after a sign, and there will be no sign given to it, except the sign of the prophet Jonah.”

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa maaringire iria, arutwo ake magĩkorwo nĩmariganĩirwo nĩgũkuua mĩgate.
English: The disciples came to the other side and had forgotten to take bread.

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Mwĩmenyererei, na mwĩhũgage ndawa ya kũimbia mĩgate ya Afarisai na Asadukai.”
English: Jesus said to them, “Take heed and beware of the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”

Kikuyu: Nao makĩaranĩria, makiuga atĩrĩ, “Hihi nĩ tondũ tũtinoka na mĩgate.”
English: They reasoned among themselves, saying, “We brought no bread.”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ aamenya ũrĩa meeranaga, akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Inyuĩ a wĩtĩkio mũnini, nĩ kĩĩ kĩratũma mwaranĩrie ũhoro wa kwaga mĩgate?
English: Jesus, perceiving it, said, “Why do you reason among yourselves, you of little faith, because you have brought no bread?

Kikuyu: Kaĩ mũtarĩ mwataũkĩrwo? Mũtingĩririkana mĩgate ĩtano ĩrĩa yarĩirwo nĩ andũ ngiri ithano, na nĩ ciondo ciigana muonganirie?
English: Don’t you yet perceive or remember the five loaves for the five thousand, and how many baskets you took up,

Kikuyu: Kana mĩgate mũgwanja ĩrĩa yarĩirwo nĩ andũ ngiri inya, na nĩ ciondo ciigana muonganirie?
English: or the seven loaves for the four thousand, and how many baskets you took up?

Kikuyu: Nĩ kĩĩ kĩragiria mũmenye
English: How is it that you don’t perceive that I didn’t speak to you concerning bread? But beware of the yeast of the Pharisees and Sadducees.”

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo makĩmenya atĩ ndaameeraga mehũũge nĩ ũndũ wa ndawa ya kũimbia mĩgate, no nĩ memenyagĩrĩre ũrutani wa Afarisai na Asadukai. Petero Kuumbũra Kristũ
English: Then they understood that he didn’t tell them to beware of the yeast of bread, but of the teaching of the Pharisees and Sadducees.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa Jesũ aakinyire rũgongo rwa Kaisarea-Filipi, nĩoririe arutwo ake atĩrĩ, “Andũ moigaga Mũrũ wa Mũndũ nũũ?”
English: Now when Jesus came into the parts of Caesarea Philippi, he asked his disciples, saying, “Who do men say that I, the Son of Man, am?”

Kikuyu: Nao makĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Amwe moigaga atĩ nĩwe Johana Mũbatithania; angĩ makoiga nĩwe Elija, o na angĩ makoiga nĩwe Jeremia, kana ũmwe wa anabii.”
English: They said, “Some say John the Baptizer, some, Elijah, and others, Jeremiah or one of the prophets.”

Kikuyu: Nake akĩmooria atĩrĩ, “Inyuĩ na inyuĩ muugaga atĩa? Muugaga niĩ nĩ niĩ ũ?”
English: He said to them, “But who do you say that I am?”

Kikuyu: Simoni Petero akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Wee nĩwe Kristũ, Mũrũ wa Ngai ũrĩa wĩ muoyo.”
English: Simon Peter answered, “You are the Christ, the Son of the living God.”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Kũrathimwo-rĩ, nĩwe Simoni mũrũ wa Jona, nĩgũkorwo ndũguũrĩirio ũndũ ũyũ nĩ mũndũ, no nĩ Baba ũrĩa ũrĩ igũrũ.
English: Jesus answered him, “Blessed are you, Simon Bar Jonah, for flesh and blood has not revealed this to you, but my Father who is in heaven.

Kikuyu: Na niĩ ngũkwĩra atĩrĩ, wee nĩwe Petero, na igũrũ rĩa ihiga rĩrĩ nĩngaka kanitha wakwa, nacio ihingo cia kwa ngoma itikaũtooria.
English: I also tell you that you are Peter,† and on this rock ‡ I will build my assembly, and the gates of Hades§ will not prevail against it.

Kikuyu: Nĩngakũnengera cabi cia ũthamaki wa igũrũ; na kĩrĩa gĩothe ũkooha gũkũ thĩ nĩgĩkoohwo igũrũ, na kĩrĩa gĩothe ũkoohora gũkũ thĩ, nĩgĩkoohorwo igũrũ.”
English: I will give to you the keys of the Kingdom of Heaven, and whatever you bind on earth will have been bound in heaven; and whatever you release on earth will have been released in heaven.”

Kikuyu: Agĩcooka agĩkaania arutwo ake matikeere mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ atĩ we nĩwe Kristũ. Jesũ Kuumbũra Ũhoro wa Gĩkuũ Gĩake
English: Then he commanded the disciples that they should tell no one that he was Jesus the Christ.

Kikuyu: Kuuma hĩndĩ ĩyo Jesũ akĩambĩrĩria gũtaarĩria arutwo ake atĩ no nginya athiĩ Jerusalemu, na athĩĩnio mũno nĩ athuuri, na athĩnjĩri-Ngai arĩa anene, na arutani a watho, na atĩ no nginya ooragwo, na thuutha wa mĩthenya ĩtatũ ariũke.
English: From that time, Jesus began to show his disciples that he must go to Jerusalem and suffer many things from the elders, chief priests, and scribes, and be killed, and the third day be raised up.

Kikuyu: Nake Petero akĩmũtwara keheri-inĩ, akĩmũkaania, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Mwathani, kũroaga gũtuĩka ũguo! Ũndũ ũcio ndũkanagũkore!”
English: Peter took him aside and began to rebuke him, saying, “Far be it from you, Lord! This will never be done to you.”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩĩhũgũrĩraPetero, akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Cooka thuutha wakwa, Shaitani! Wee wĩ mũhĩnga harĩ niĩ; wee ndwĩciiragia maũndũ moimĩte kũrĩ Ngai, tiga maũndũ moimĩte kũrĩ andũ.”
English: But he turned and said to Peter, “Get behind me, Satan! You are a stumbling block to me, for you are not setting your mind on the things of God, but on the things of men.”

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo Jesũ akĩĩra arutwo ake atĩrĩ, “Mũndũ o wothe ũngĩenda kũnũmĩrĩra, no nginya eerege we mwene, oe mũtharaba wake aanũmĩrĩre.
English: Then Jesus said to his disciples, “If anyone desires to come after me, let him deny himself, take up his cross, and follow me.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo mũndũ ũrĩa wothe wendaga kũhonokia muoyo wake nĩakoorwo nĩguo; no ũrĩa wothe ũkoorwo nĩ muoyo wake nĩ ũndũ wakwa nĩakaũhonokia.
English: For whoever desires to save his life will lose it, and whoever will lose his life for my sake will find it.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ-rĩ, mũndũ angĩĩguna nakĩ o na angĩĩgwatĩra thĩ yothe, na orwo nĩ muoyo wake? Kana nĩ kĩĩ mũndũ angĩkũũrania muoyo wake nakĩo?
English: For what will it profit a man if he gains the whole world and forfeits his life? Or what will a man give in exchange for his life?

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Mũrũ wa Mũndũ nĩagooka na riiri wa Ithe hamwe na araika ake, na hĩndĩ ĩyo nĩakahe o mũndũ kĩheo gĩake kũringana na ciĩko ciake.
English: For the Son of Man will come in the glory of his Father with his angels, and then he will give to everyone according to his deeds.

Kikuyu: Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, amwe marũngiĩ haha matigacama gĩkuũ matonete Mũrũ wa Mũndũ agĩũka na ũthamaki wake.”
English: Most certainly I tell you, there are some standing here who will in no way taste of death until they see the Son of Man coming in his Kingdom.”

Kikuyu: huutha wa mĩthenya ĩtandatũ, Jesũ nĩathiire na Petero, na Jakubu, na mũrũ wa nyina Johana, akĩmatwara kĩrĩma-inĩ kĩraaya handũ hataarĩ na andũ.
English: fter six days, Jesus took with him Peter, James, and John his brother, and brought them up into a high mountain by themselves.

Kikuyu: Arĩ hau mbere yao akĩgarũrũka ũrĩa aahaanaga. Ũthiũ wake ũkĩara ta riũa, na nguo ciake ikĩerũha cua, ta ũtheri.
English: He was changed† before them. His face shone like the sun, and his garments became as white as the light.

Kikuyu: O hĩndĩ ĩyo hau mbere yao makiumĩrĩrwo nĩ Musa na Elija makĩaranĩria na Jesũ.
English: Behold, Moses and Elijah appeared to them talking with him.

Kikuyu: Nake Petero akĩĩra Jesũ atĩrĩ, “Mwathani, nĩ wega tũikare gũkũ; ũngĩenda-rĩ, no njake ithũnũ ithatũ, kĩmwe gĩaku, na kĩmwe kĩa Musa, na kĩrĩa kĩngĩ kĩa Elija.”
English: Peter answered and said to Jesus, “Lord, it is good for us to be here. If you want, let’s make three tents here: one for you, one for Moses, and one for Elijah.”  Peter’s name, Petros in Greek, is the word for a specific rock or stone.  Greek, petra, a rock mass or

Kikuyu: O akĩaragia-rĩ, itu ikengu rĩgĩũka rĩkĩmahumbĩra, na mũgambo ũkiuma itu-inĩ rĩu ũkiuga atĩrĩ, “Ũyũ nĩwe Mũrũ wakwa ũrĩa nyendete; na nĩngenagio mũno nĩwe. Mũiguagei!”
English: While he was still speaking, behold, a bright cloud overshadowed them. Behold, a voice came out of the cloud, saying, “This is my beloved Son, in whom I am well pleased. Listen to him.”

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa arutwo maiguire ũguo-rĩ, makĩinamĩrĩra, magĩturumithia mothiũ mao thĩ mamakĩte mũno.
English: When the disciples heard it, they fell on their faces, and were very afraid.

Kikuyu: No Jesũ agĩũka, akĩmahutia na akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Arahũkai, tigai gwĩtigĩra.”
English: Jesus came and touched them and said, “Get up, and don’t be afraid.”

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩa maatiirire maitho-rĩ, mationire mũndũ ũngĩ tiga o Jesũ wiki.
English: Lifting up their eyes, they saw no one, except Jesus alone.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩa maikũrũkaga kuuma kĩrĩma-inĩ, Jesũ akĩmaatha akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Mũtikae kwĩra mũndũ o na ũrĩkũ ũrĩa muona, nginya rĩrĩa Mũrũ wa Mũndũ akaariũkio oime kũrĩ arĩa akuũ.”
English: As they were coming down from the mountain, Jesus commanded them, saying, “Don’t tell anyone what you saw, until the Son of Man has risen from the dead.”

Kikuyu: Nao arutwo makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Nĩkĩ gĩtũmaga arutani a watho moige atĩ Elija no nginya aambe ooke?”
English: His disciples asked him, saying, “Then why do the scribes say that Elijah must come first?”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Ti-itherũ, Elija no nginya aambe ooke, ahaarĩrie maũndũ mothe.
English: Jesus answered them, “Elijah indeed comes first, and will restore all things;

Kikuyu: No ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, Elija nĩarĩkĩtie gũũka, no andũ makĩaga kũmũmenya, no nĩmamwĩkire ũrĩa wothe meendaga kũmwĩka. O ũguo noguo nake Mũrũ wa Mũndũ akaanyariirwo nĩo.”
English: but I tell you that Elijah has come already, and they didn’t recognize him, but did to him whatever they wanted to. Even so the Son of Man will also suffer by them.”

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩyo arutwo makĩmenya atĩ aamaheaga ũhoro wa Johana Mũbatithania. Kũhonia Kamwana kaarĩ na Ndaimono
English: Then the disciples understood that he spoke to them of John the Baptizer.

Kikuyu: Rĩrĩamaakinyireharĩakĩrĩndĩkĩarĩ, mũndũũmweagĩthiĩharĩJesũ, akĩmũturĩriandu,
English: When they came to the multitude, a man came to him, kneeling down to him and saying,

Kikuyu: akĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, “Mwathani iguĩra mũrũ wakwa tha, arĩ na mũrimũ wa kĩbaba na nĩũmũthĩĩnagia mũno. Kaingĩ nĩagũũaga mwaki-inĩ kana akagũa maaĩ-inĩ.
English: “Lord, have mercy on my son, for he is epileptic and suffers grievously; for he often falls into the fire, and often into the water.

Kikuyu: Nĩndĩramũreheire arutwo aku, no matinahota kũmũhonia.”
English: So I brought him to your disciples, and they could not cure him.”

Kikuyu: Nake Jesũ akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Rũciaro rũrũ rũtetĩkĩtie na rũremi, nĩ nginya rĩ ngũikara na inyuĩ? Ngũmũkirĩrĩria nginya rĩ? Ndeherai kamwana kau.”
English: Jesus answered, “Faithless and perverse generation! How long will I be with you? How long will I bear with you? Bring him here to me.”

Kikuyu: Jesũ agĩkũũma ndaimono ĩyo, nayo ĩkiuma kamwana kau, na gakĩhonio o hĩndĩ ĩyo.
English: Jesus rebuked the demon, and it went out of him, and the boy was cured from that hour.

Kikuyu: Nao arutwo magĩthiĩ kũrĩ Jesũ keheri-inĩ, makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Tũremirwo nĩkũingata ndaimono ĩyo nĩkĩ?”
English: Then the disciples came to Jesus privately, and said, “Why weren’t we able to cast it out?”

Kikuyu: Nake akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ tondũ wĩtĩkio wanyu nĩ mũnini. Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, mũngĩkorwo na wĩtĩkio mũnini ta mbeũ ya karatarĩ, no mũhote kwĩra kĩrĩma gĩkĩ atĩrĩ, ‘Ehera haha, ũthiĩ haarĩa’ na gĩthiĩ. Gũtirĩ ũndũ ũkaamũrema.” (
English: He said to them, “Because of your unbelief. For most certainly I tell you, if you have faith as a grain of mustard seed, you will tell this mountain, ‘Move from here to there,’ and it will move; and nothing will be impossible for you.

Kikuyu: No ndaimono mũthemba ũcio ndĩngĩingatĩka na kĩndũ kĩngĩ, tiga mahooya na kwĩhinga kũrĩa irio.”)
English: But this kind doesn’t go out except by prayer and fasting.” ‡

Kikuyu: Hĩndĩ ĩrĩa macookanĩrĩire kũu Galili, akĩmeera atĩrĩ, “Mũrũ wa Mũndũ nĩegũkunyanĩrwo aneanwo moko-inĩ ma andũ.
English: While they were staying in Galilee, Jesus said to them, “The Son of Man is about to be delivered up into the hands of men,

Kikuyu: Nĩmakamũũraga, na mũthenya wa ĩtatũ nĩakariũkio.” Nao arutwo makĩiyũrwo nĩ kĩeha. Igooti rĩa Hekarũ
English: and they will kill him, and the third day he will be raised up.” They were exceedingly sorry.

Kikuyu: Na rĩrĩ, Jesũ na arutwo ake maarĩkia gũkinya Kaperinaumu, arĩa meetagia andũ daraki igĩrĩ cia igooti rĩa hekarũ magĩthiĩ kũrĩ Petero, makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Kaĩ mũrutani wanyu atarutaga igooti rĩa hekarũ?”
English: When they had come to Capernaum, those who collected the didrachma coins§ came to Peter, and said, “Doesn’t your teacher pay the didrachma?”

Kikuyu: Nake akĩmacookeria atĩrĩ, “Ĩĩ, nĩarutaga.” Rĩrĩa Petero aatoonyire nyũmba, Jesũ akĩmũhĩta kwaria akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Ũgwĩciiria atĩa, wee Simoni? Athamaki a thĩ-rĩ, metagia igooti kuuma kũrĩ a? Metagia ariũ ao, kana metagia andũ arĩa angĩ?”
English: He said, “Yes.” When he came into the house, Jesus anticipated him, saying, “What do you think, Simon? From whom do the kings of the earth receive toll or tribute? From their children, or from strangers?”

Kikuyu: Nake Petero akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ kuuma kũrĩ andũ arĩa angĩ.” Nake Jesũ akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio ariũ ao matirĩhaga.
English: Peter said to him, “From strangers.” Jesus said to him, “Therefore the children are exempt.

Kikuyu: No nĩguo tũtikamarakarie-rĩ, thiĩ iria-inĩ ũikie ndwano kuo, na thamaki ĩrĩa ũkũnyiita mbere, ũmĩtumũre kanua kayo, na nĩũkuona mbeeca, daraki inya; cioe ũthiĩ ũrĩhe igooti rĩakwa na rĩaku.”
English: But, lest we cause them to stumble, go to the sea, cast a hook, and take up the first fish that comes up. When you have opened its mouth, you will find a stater coin.† Take that, and give it to them for me and you.”

Kikuyu: hinda o rĩu arutwo magĩũka kũrĩ Jesũ, makĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Nũũ mũnene gũkĩra arĩa angĩ ũthamaki-inĩ wa igũrũ?”
English: n that hour the disciples came to Jesus, saying, “Who then is greatest in the Kingdom of Heaven?”

Kikuyu: Nake agĩĩta kaana, agĩkarũgamia gatagatĩ kao.
English: Jesus called a little child to himself, and set him in the middle of them

Kikuyu: Agĩcooka akiuga atĩrĩ, “Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, tiga mũgarũrũkire mũtuĩke o ta twana, mũtigatoonya ũthamaki wa igũrũ.
English: and said, “Most certainly I tell you, unless you turn and become as little  NU omits verse 21.  A didrachma is a Greek silver coin worth 2 drachmas, about as much as 2 Roman denarii, or about 2 days’ wages. It was commonly used to pay the half-shekel temple tax, because 2 drachmas were worth one half shekel of silver. A shekel is about 10 grams or about 0.35 ounces.  A stater is a silver coin equivalent to four Attic or two Alexandrian drachmas, or a Jewish shekel: just exactly enough to cover the half-shekel temple tax for two people. A shekel is about 10 grams or about 0.35 ounces, usually in the form of a silver coin.

Kikuyu: Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, ũrĩa wothe wĩnyiihagia ta kaana gaka nĩwe mũnene gũkĩra arĩa angĩ ũthamaki-inĩ wa igũrũ.
English: Whoever therefore humbles himself as this little child is the greatest in the Kingdom of Heaven.

Kikuyu: “Nake ũrĩa wothe wamũkagĩra kaana ta gaka thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩakwa, nĩ niĩ aamũkagĩra.
English: Whoever receives one such little child in my name receives me,

Kikuyu: No ũrĩa wothe ũngĩhĩtithia kamwe ga twana tũtũ tũnjĩtĩkĩtie keehie, nĩ kaba mũndũ ũcio aharũkio ihiga rĩa gĩthĩi ngingo aikio iria-inĩ kũrĩa kũriku.
English: but whoever causes one of these little ones who believe in me to stumble, it would be better for him if a huge millstone were hung around his neck and that he were sunk in the depths of the sea.

Kikuyu: “Kaĩ gũkũ thĩ kũrĩ na haaro-ĩ, nĩ ũndũ wa maũndũ marĩa matũmaga andũ mehie! Maũndũ ta macio no nginya moke, no mũndũ ũrĩa ũtũmaga moke kaĩ arĩ na haaro-ĩ!
English: “Woe to the world because of occasions of stumbling! For it must be that the occasions come, but woe to that person through whom the occasion comes!

Kikuyu: Guoko gwaku kana kũgũrũ gwaku kũngĩtũma wĩhie-rĩ, gũtinie ũgũte. Nĩ kaba ũtoonye muoyo-inĩ wĩ gĩthua kana wĩ kĩonje, gũkĩra ũkorwo na moko meerĩ kana magũrũ meerĩ na ũikio mwaki-inĩ wa tene na tene.
English: If your hand or your foot causes you to stumble, cut it off and cast it from you. It is better for you to enter into life maimed or crippled, rather than having two hands or two feet to be cast into the eternal fire.

Kikuyu: Narĩo riitho rĩaku rĩngĩtũma wĩhie-rĩ, rĩkũũre, ũrĩte. Nĩ kaba ũtoonye muoyo-inĩ na riitho rĩmwe, gũkĩra ũkorwo na maitho meerĩ na ũikio mwaki-inĩ wa kwa ngoma.
English: If your eye causes you to stumble, pluck it out and cast it from you. It is better for you to enter into life with one eye, rather than having two eyes to be cast into the Gehenna† of fire.

Kikuyu: “Menyererai mũtikae kũnyarara o na kamwe ga twana tũtũ tũnini. Nĩgũkorwo ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, araika a tuo nĩmonaga ũthiũ wa Baba ũrĩa ũrĩ igũrũ, hĩndĩ ciothe.” (
English: See that you don’t despise one of these little ones, for I tell you that in heaven their angels always see the face of my Father who is in heaven.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo Mũrũ wa Mũndũ ookire kũhonokia kĩrĩa kĩorĩte.)
English: For the Son of Man came to save that which was lost.‡

Kikuyu: “Rĩu-rĩ, mũgwĩciiria atĩa? Mũndũ angĩkorwo arĩ na ngʼondu igana rĩmwe, na ĩmwe yacio yũre- rĩ, githĩ ndangĩtiga icio mĩrongo kenda na kenda kũu irĩma-inĩ, athiĩ agacarie ĩyo yũrĩte?
English: “What do you think? If a man has one hundred sheep, and one of them goes astray, doesn’t he leave the ninety-nine, go to the mountains, and seek that which has gone astray?

Kikuyu: Na angĩmĩona-rĩ, ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, akenagĩra ngʼondu ĩyo ĩmwe ĩrorĩte kũrĩ ũrĩa akenagĩra icio mĩrongo kenda na kenda itaroorĩte.
English: If he finds it, most certainly I tell you, he rejoices over it more than over the ninety-nine which have not gone astray.

Kikuyu: Ũguo noguo Ithe wanyu ũrĩa ũrĩ Igũrũ atangĩenda o na kamwe ga twana tũtũ tũnini koore. Mũndũ Wanyu Akũhĩtĩirie
English: Even so it is not the will of your Father who is in heaven that one of these little ones should perish.

Kikuyu: “Mũrũ kana mwarĩ wa thoguo angĩkũhĩtĩria-rĩ, thiĩ ũmuonie ihĩtia rĩake mũrĩ o inyuĩ eerĩ. Angĩgũthikĩrĩria-rĩ, nĩmwacookanwo nake.
English: “If your brother sins against you, go, show him his fault between you and him alone. If he listens to you, you have gained back your brother.

Kikuyu: No angĩrega gũgũthikĩrĩria, oya mũndũ ũngĩ ũmwe kana eerĩ mũthiĩ nao, nĩguo ũndũ o wothe ũrũgamagĩrĩrwo nĩ ũira wa andũ eerĩ kana atatũ.
English: But if he doesn’t listen, take one or two more with you, that at the mouth of two or three witnesses every word may be established.*

Kikuyu: Angĩrega kũmathikĩrĩria-rĩ, ĩra kanitha ũndũ ũcio; na angĩkĩrega gũthikĩrĩria o na kanitha-rĩ, mũtue o ta mũndũ ũtetĩkĩtie kana ta mwĩtia wa mbeeca cia igooti.
English: If he refuses to listen to them, tell it to the assembly. If he refuses to hear the assembly also, let him be to you as a Gentile or a tax collector.

Kikuyu: “Ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ na ma, ũndũ o wothe mũkooha gũkũ thĩ, o na igũrũ nĩũkoohwo, na ũrĩa wothe mũkoohora gũkũ thĩ, o na igũrũ nĩũkohorwo.
English: Most certainly I tell you, whatever things you bind on earth will have been bound in heaven, and whatever things you release on earth will have been released in heaven.

Kikuyu: “O rĩngĩ ngũmwĩra atĩrĩ, andũ eerĩ anyu mangĩiguanĩra gũkũ thĩ ũndũ o wothe ũrĩa marahooya, nĩmakahingĩrio nĩ Baba ũrĩa ũrĩ igũrũ.
English: Again, assuredly I tell you, that if two of you will agree on earth concerning anything that they will ask, it will be done for them by my Father who is in heaven.

Kikuyu: Nĩgũkorwo harĩa andũ eerĩ kana atatũ monganĩte thĩinĩ wa rĩĩtwa rĩakwa-rĩ, ngoragwo ndĩ ho gatagatĩ kao.” Ngerekano ya Ndungata ĩtarĩ Tha
English: For where two or three are gathered together in my name, there I am in the middle of them.”

Kikuyu: Ningĩ Petero agĩũka harĩ Jesũ, akĩmũũria atĩrĩ, “Mwathani, mũrũ kana mwarĩ wa baba angĩhĩtĩria maita maigana ngĩmũrekagĩra? Hihi nĩ maita mũgwanja?”
English: Then Peter came and said to him, “Lord, how often shall my brother sin against me, and I forgive him? Until seven times?”

Kikuyu: Jesũ akĩmũcookeria atĩrĩ, “Ngũkwĩra atĩrĩ, ti maita mũgwanja, no nĩ mĩrongo mũgwanja, maita mũgwanja.
English: Jesus said to him, “I don’t tell you until seven times, but, until seventy times seven.

Kikuyu: “Nĩ ũndũ ũcio, ũthamaki wa igũrũ ũhaananĩtio na mũthamaki ũrĩa wendaga kuonio mathabu ma mbeeca ciake nĩ ndungata ciake.
English: Therefore the Kingdom of Heaven is like a certain king who wanted to settle accounts with his servants.

Kikuyu: Aambĩrĩria kũrora mathabu-rĩ, mũndũ warĩ na thiirĩ wake wa taranda ngiri ikũmi* akĩrehwo kũrĩ we.
English: When he had begun to settle, one was brought to him who owed him ten thousand talents.§

Kikuyu: Na tondũ nĩaremetwo nĩ kũrĩha-rĩ, mwathi wake agĩathana atĩ mũndũ ũcio, endio hamwe na mũtumia wake, na ciana ciake, na kĩrĩa gĩothe aarĩ nakĩo nĩguo thiirĩ ũrĩhĩke.
English: But because he couldn’t pay, his lord commanded him to be sold, with his wife, his children, and all that he had, and payment to be made.

Kikuyu: “Ndungata ĩyo ĩkĩĩgũithia mbere yake, ĩkĩmũthaitha, ĩkĩmwĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Ngirĩrĩria, na nĩndĩĩkũrĩha thiirĩ wothe.’
English: The servant therefore fell down and knelt before him, saying, ‘Lord, have patience with me, and I will repay you all!’

Kikuyu: Nake mwathi ũcio wa ndungata ĩyo akĩmĩiguĩra tha, akĩmĩrekera thiirĩ ũcio akĩreka ĩthiĩ.
English: The lord of that servant, being moved with compassion, released him and forgave him the debt.

Kikuyu: “No ndungata ĩyo yoima nja, ĩgĩcemania na ndungata ĩngĩ yarutaga wĩra nayo yarĩ na thiirĩ wayo wa dinari igana rĩmwe.† Ĩkĩmĩnyiita mũmero ĩmĩite, ĩkĩmĩĩra na hinya atĩrĩ, ‘Ndĩha thiirĩ wakwa ũrĩa ũrĩ naguo!’
English: “But that servant went out and found one of his fellow servants who owed him one hundred denarii,† and he grabbed him and took him by the throat, saying, ‘Pay me what you owe!’  or, Hell  NU omits verse 11.  Deuteronomy 19:15  Ten thousand talents (about 300 metric tons of silver) represents an extremely large sum of money, equivalent to about 60,000,000 denarii, where one denarius was typical of one day’s wages for agricultural labor.  100 denarii was about one sixtieth of a talent, or about 500 grams (1.1 pounds) of silver.

Kikuyu: “Ndungata ĩyo ĩkĩĩgũithia thĩ, ĩkĩmĩthaitha, ĩkĩmĩĩra atĩrĩ, ‘Ngirĩrĩria, na nĩndĩĩkũrĩha.’
English: “So his fellow servant fell down at his feet and begged him, saying, ‘Have patience with me, and I will repay you!’
